title: abort on assert(co->_co_unique_id == _Py_INVALID_UNIQUE_ID) in code_dealloc (Objects/codeobject.c) when init_code() fails under MemoryError
description: `Objects/codeobject.c`: `_co_unique_id` is left uninitialized when `init_code()` fails under OOM (it is only assigned after success in `_PyCode_New`), so `code_dealloc`'s free-threaded assert fires on garbage and aborts
notes: Free-threading-specific (_co_unique_id + assert gated on Py_GIL_DISABLED). assert-based abort: fires only on the FT debug build (release defines NDEBUG), so ft_release/jit/upstream are n/a. init_code (codeobject.c:511) never sets _co_unique_id; _PyCode_New assigns it at L752 only after init_code succeeds, so when init_code's _PyCodeArray_New (tlbc, L570) fails under OOM the Py_DECREF(co) at L747 deallocs with garbage _co_unique_id. Fix: initialize co->_co_unique_id = _Py_INVALID_UNIQUE_ID before any code_dealloc-reachable path. Repro drives _PyCode_New via marshal.loads; original vehicles reach it via importlib _compile_bytecode during stdlib import under OOM.
